semantic.role: Configuration resolution layer for the view subsystem: reads project config and environment variables to determine whether the view layer is enabled and which view IDs are active.
responsibilities: Resolve the boolean view layer state (enabled/disabled) from project config and env var SPINE_VIEW_LAYER, with fallback to default.
Resolve the set of enabled ViewIds by merging project-configured views, normalizing them, and identifying unknown IDs.
Track the source of each resolved value (project-config, env, or default) for transparency.
outOfScope: View rendering, data production, or orchestration of view tasks.
Direct access to databases or file systems.
Mutating configuration or writing state back to config.
invariants: The module does not mutate the Config object; it only reads via defined accessors.
All resolved outputs are derived from inputs; no side effects on external state.
Normalization of view IDs is delegated to view-registry functions.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a centralized, traceable resolution layer that decouples view configuration parsing from the rest of the view subsystem.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Maintenance pass aligned with release gate fixes; no semantic change to the module's contract.

publicSurface.description: Interface representing the resolved boolean state of the view layer, including the source of resolution (project-config, env, or default).
Interface representing the resolved list of enabled ViewId values, their resolution source, and any unknown IDs that were not recognized.

Reads the view layer setting from project config (via getViewLayer) and env var SPINE_VIEW_LAYER, then returns a ResolvedViewLayer with source tracking. Called during system startup to decide whether the view system is active.

Reads the configured enabled views from project config, normalizes them using view-registry, falls back to defaults, and returns a ResolvedEnabledViews with source and unknown IDs. Called to determine which specific views are active.
